name: E-commerce / marketplace / payments
summary: A transactional commerce system where users browse a catalog, add to cart, and pay, with money, inventory, and fulfilment as systems of record. Correctness of payment and inventory, fraud resistance, and survival of seasonal traffic peaks dominate; marketplaces add multi-party settlement and trust.

concerns: PCI-DSS 4.0.1 scope minimization via tokenization / hosted fields / wallets so the app never handles PAN
payment-page script integrity and change monitoring (PCI 6.4.3 / 11.6.1); SAQ-A vs SAQ-A-EP scoping
fraud prevention: velocity checks, device fingerprinting, address/CVV verification, 3-D Secure / SCA (PSD2)
account-takeover, credential-stuffing, and carding/BIN-attack protection on login and payment endpoints
coupon/promo and price-manipulation abuse; parameter tampering on cart totals
PII and payment-token protection, secure secrets and key rotation
questions: Is PCI-DSS scope minimized (tokenization/hosted fields/wallets) so cardholder data never touches your systems, and is the SAQ level identified?
Are fraud controls (SCA/3-DS, velocity, device fingerprinting) and carding/ATO protections specified?
Are cart totals, prices, and promos validated server-side against tampering?

concerns: checkout integrity: totals = items + tax + shipping - discounts, computed authoritatively server-side
order state machine: pending -> authorized -> captured -> fulfilled -> refunded/cancelled, with valid transitions only
payment lifecycle: auth vs capture, partial capture, voids, chargebacks, and idempotent retries
cart/session semantics: merge on login, expiry, price/availability revalidation at checkout
returns, refunds (full/partial), exchanges, store credit, and their inventory/ledger effects
tax calculation (VAT/GST/sales tax by jurisdiction) and shipping rate/method selection
promotions: stacking rules, eligibility, minimums, per-customer/global usage limits
questions: Is order/payment totals and state-machine transitions specified authoritatively (server-side), including auth/capture/void/refund?
Are cart merge, expiry, and price/availability revalidation at checkout defined?
Are refund/return/exchange flows and their inventory and ledger effects specified?

concerns: inventory consistency: oversell prevention, reservations/holds, atomic decrement under concurrency
catalog model: products, variants/SKUs, options, pricing (list/sale/tiered), currency, availability
order/financial ledger accuracy and reconciliation with the payment processor and accounting
search/faceting index kept consistent with catalog (denormalized read model)
customer, address, and payment-token data with retention and PII classification
marketplace: seller catalogs, inventory ownership, and settlement/payout records
questions: Is inventory consistency under concurrency specified to prevent oversell (reservations, atomic decrement)?
Is the order/payment ledger reconciled against the processor, and are discrepancies handled?
Is the catalog/variant/pricing model and its search-index consistency defined?

concerns: checkout must not double-charge or lose orders on retry/timeout (idempotency keys, dedup)
graceful degradation when payment/fraud/tax/shipping providers are slow or down (queue, fallback, allow-browse)
high availability during revenue-critical windows; no lost orders on partial failure
backups, RTO/RPO for order and payment data; exactly-once payment capture
webhook reliability from processors (retries, ordering, replay) with idempotent handlers
questions: Is checkout idempotent so timeouts/retries never double-charge or drop an order?
Is graceful degradation specified when payment/tax/shipping providers fail?
Are processor webhooks handled idempotently with retry/replay tolerance?

concerns: seasonal/flash-sale peak load (Black Friday, drops) many multiples over baseline; autoscaling & load-shedding
catalog browse and faceted search latency at scale; caching and CDN for product pages/images
cart and checkout latency under contention on hot inventory (popular SKUs, single-item drops)
queueing/virtual-waiting-room for extreme spikes to protect checkout integrity
conversion sensitivity to page speed (Core Web Vitals) on product and checkout pages
questions: Are peak/seasonal load targets and the scaling/load-shedding strategy quantified?
Is browse/search latency and caching/CDN strategy specified at catalog scale?
Is contention on hot inventory (drops/flash sales) handled without oversell or checkout collapse?

concerns: consumer-protection law: pricing transparency, right of withdrawal/returns, receipts/invoicing
cross-border tax obligations (VAT MOSS/OSS, marketplace facilitator tax, US nexus)
GDPR/CCPA consent, cookie/tracking consent, and marketing-consent management
strong customer authentication (PSD2/SCA) obligations in applicable regions
marketplace: KYC/AML on sellers, payout/settlement compliance, seller tax reporting (e.g. 1099-K/DAC7)
questions: Are consumer-protection obligations (pricing transparency, withdrawal/returns, invoicing) captured?
Are cross-border tax and, for marketplaces, KYC/AML and seller tax-reporting obligations specified?
Are consent/cookie and SCA (PSD2) requirements defined for the target regions?

concerns: order-management/fulfilment operations, dispatch, tracking, and exception handling (stuck orders, failed payments)
monitoring of the revenue funnel and payment-success/decline rates with alerting
safe deploys during peak periods (change freezes, feature flags, canary)
catalog/price/promo publishing pipeline and merchandising operations
refund/dispute/chargeback operational workflows
questions: Are order-management, fulfilment, and stuck-order/failed-payment exception workflows specified?
Is monitoring of payment-success and funnel metrics with alerting defined?
Is change control (freezes, canary) around peak periods specified?

concerns: low-friction, guest-capable checkout to minimize abandonment; minimal steps and clear cost breakdown
trust signals: security badges, clear pricing/shipping/returns before payment, no surprise fees
product discovery: search relevance, faceted filtering, recommendations
error recovery on payment decline/validation without losing cart
mobile-first commerce ergonomics and wallet/express-checkout (Apple Pay/Google Pay/one-click)
questions: Is checkout friction minimized (guest checkout, express wallets) and total cost shown before payment?
Is payment-decline/error recovery specified without losing the cart?
Are search relevance and product-discovery expectations stated?

concerns: payment-gateway/PSP integrations and method coverage (cards, wallets, BNPL, local methods)
tax, shipping-carrier, fraud, and ERP/OMS/accounting integrations via versioned APIs and webhooks
product-feed syndication to channels/marketplaces (Google Shopping, Amazon) and their schemas
marketplace seller-integration APIs and inventory feeds
questions: Are the payment, tax, shipping, and ERP/OMS integrations and their versioned contracts specified?
Are supported payment methods and channel/feed syndication requirements defined?

concerns: conversion-rate and cart-abandonment outcomes tied to funnel design
checkout completion time and effort to purchase
buyer trust/satisfaction and, for marketplaces, two-sided trust (ratings, dispute resolution)
freedom from economic risk: no incorrect charges, clear refunds
questions: Are conversion, abandonment, and time-to-purchase outcome metrics defined?
For marketplaces, are two-sided trust and dispute-resolution outcomes specified?
